name: arq
what_it_does: Fast async job queue for Python using Redis. arq is designed for asyncio applications where celery feels too heavy — enqueue async Python coroutines as background jobs with Redis as the broker. Much simpler than Celery: no separate broker config, native async, and minimal boilerplate. Used by FastAPI applications that need lightweight background task processing.
use_cases: Background job processing for agent APIs — enqueue slow operations (LLM calls, data processing) without blocking requests
Scheduled and deferred agent tasks — run jobs at specific times or after a delay using arq's scheduler
Async worker pools for parallel agent task execution with controlled concurrency limits
Lightweight alternative to Celery for FastAPI agent services that already use Redis
Retry-capable background processing for agent workflows with configurable retry counts and delays
not_for: Synchronous Python applications — arq is async-only; use Celery or RQ for sync workers
Complex workflow DAGs — use Prefect or Celery Chord for complex task dependencies
Applications without Redis — arq requires Redis as its only supported broker
best_when: You're building a FastAPI/asyncio application that needs lightweight background job processing and already uses Redis — simpler than Celery for async Python.
avoid_when: You need synchronous workers, complex task DAGs, or a broker other than Redis — use Celery or Dramatiq.

auth.notes: Auth via Redis connection string (password, TLS). arq itself has no auth layer.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by Redis throughput and worker concurrency settings

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Job failures include traceback in Redis. arq.exceptions.Retry for explicit retry. Worker logs job errors with context. Limited built-in monitoring UI.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job deduplication requires application-level unique job IDs. arq supports job_id parameter for explicit deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: arq workers must be started as separate processes — they don't auto-start; deployment requires a separate worker process alongside the FastAPI app
Job serialization uses msgpack — function arguments must be msgpack-serializable; custom objects require custom serialization
Job deduplication: enqueuing a job with the same job_id won't create duplicates — but different job_id (default) creates new job every time
arq doesn't have a built-in monitoring UI — use arq's programmatic API or external Redis monitoring for job visibility
Scheduled jobs (cron_jobs) are defined in WorkerSettings — changing schedules requires worker restart, not live update
Job timeouts must be configured in WorkerSettings.job_timeout — there's no per-job timeout override without creating separate worker classes

security_score_components.security_notes: Redis TLS and auth supported. Job arguments serialized to Redis — avoid storing sensitive data in job payloads. No built-in access control for job inspection.
